data_scope: human and mouse; 5,313 human and 1,643 mouse functional tracks
context: 196,608 bp input; 114,688 bp output at 128-bp bins
architecture: 7 convolution/pooling blocks plus 11 transformer blocks; 1,536 channels; relative position encoding
training_compute: 64 TPU v3 cores; batch 64; 150,000 steps; about 3 days
split_or_leakage_control: 1-Mb human-mouse syntenic connected components kept in the same train/validation/test partition
key_evidence: long-range context improved CAGE correlation and enhancer-promoter modeling
limitation: assay/cell-type scope is limited to supervised training labels; costly full-context model

data_scope: 3,202 human genomes or 850 diverse RefSeq species; multispecies set totals 174 billion nt and explicitly excludes plants and viruses
objective: BERT MLM; 15% selected tokens, later 20% for v2
architecture: encoder-only Transformer; learned positions v1, rotary positions and SwiGLU v2
training_compute: 2.5B: 128 A100 GPUs for 28 days; v2 models: one 8-A100 node for one day
split_or_leakage_control: downstream tenfold CV; no plant pretraining; published benchmark notes possible Enformer split leakage
key_evidence: multispecies diversity often helps more than size; 50M v2 can match a much larger model
limitation: 6-mer loses direct base alignment; full benchmark compute is far beyond one A100
primary_source: Nature Methods full text, Results and Methods

data_scope: 48 edible plant reference genomes; 10.5 million 6-kb sequences
training_compute: 8 A100 GPUs; bfloat16; batch 80; 100,000 steps; about 8 days
split_or_leakage_control: article reports held-out downstream test sets; no leave-genus-out pretraining evaluation extracted
key_evidence: plant-specific pretraining improves regulatory and annotation tasks versus generic DNA models
limitation: large compute, 6-kb ceiling and no explicit RC-equivariant architecture
primary_source: Communications Biology full text, Results and Methods

data_scope: seven Brassicales genomes for multispecies model; Arabidopsis thaliana excluded from pretraining
architecture: convolutional network with 30 dilated residual layers and 512 hidden units
training_compute: one A100 GPU; about 4 days per model; five seeds
split_or_leakage_control: entire target A. thaliana species held out; equal windows sampled per training genome
key_evidence: strong zero-shot plant variant-effect and genome-wide constraint signal with modest compute
limitation: short context; Brassicales-specific; zero-shot scores do not replace leakage-safe downstream tests

data_scope: 806 fungal species; more than 10 million 5-prime and 3-prime noncoding sequences
split_or_leakage_control: the entire Saccharomyces genus held out; unseen species uses a related proxy species token
key_evidence: species conditioning improves motif reconstruction and downstream linear probes at almost no token cost
limitation: short context; proxy token needed for unseen taxa; fungal regulatory regime differs from long-range plant regulation
primary_source: Genome Biology full text, Results and Methods

objective: bidirectional causal language modeling with both DNA directions
architecture: MambaDNA/Caduceus-style bidirectional state-space model with reverse-complement equivariance
training_compute: 8 H100 GPUs; 750 billion tokens; about 25 days
split_or_leakage_control: cross-species evaluations; pretraining diversity analyzed; identity-aware task controls reported
key_evidence: single-base and exact RC symmetry improve plant sequence modeling and variant prediction
limitation: 512-bp context and training cost; cannot directly model distal plant regulation
primary_source: PNAS full text, Results and Methods

data_scope: OpenGenome2 across all domains; more than 8.8 trillion nucleotides; 2.4T or 9.3T training tokens
context: 8,192-base pretraining then staged extension to 1 million bases
architecture: StripedHyena 2 multi-hybrid short, medium and long convolution operators plus attention
training_compute: paper reports 1,024-GPU scale throughput comparisons; exact total device-days not extracted
split_or_leakage_control: eukaryotic viruses excluded for biosafety; broad held-out evaluations
key_evidence: proves staged context extension and multi-scale operators can reach 1 Mb at single-base resolution
limitation: orders of magnitude beyond available compute; broad-domain scale is not a feasible project target
primary_source: Nature full text, Results and Methods

data_scope: human and mouse RNA-seq plus CAGE, DNase, ATAC and ChIP tracks
parameters: not stated for full model in extracted text
architecture: Enformer-like convolution and 8 self-attention blocks at 128 bp plus U-Net upsampling to 32 bp
training_compute: full model uses two 40-GB A100 GPUs, batch 2, about 25 days; one sequence fills 40 GB
split_or_leakage_control: orthologous human-mouse regions assigned together across eight partitions
key_evidence: long input and finer output support RNA-seq, splicing and variant interpretation
limitation: full model is at the single-A100 memory limit and training exceeds available budget
primary_source: Nature Genetics full text, Results and Methods

data_scope: human and mouse; 5,930 human and 1,128 mouse tracks across 11 modalities
context: 1 Mb input; outputs up to 1-bp resolution
parameters: not publicly stated in extracted text
objective: supervised multimodal track prediction followed by teacher-student distillation
architecture: U-Net encoder-transformer-decoder; 1-bp, 128-bp and 2,048-bp representations; sequence parallelism
training_compute: 1-Mb training spans eight interconnected TPU v3 devices per sequence; total training budget not stated
split_or_leakage_control: four genomic folds for fold-specific test; all-fold teachers only for distilled variant model
key_evidence: long context and base resolution jointly improve diverse sequence-to-function tasks
limitation: not a self-supervised crop model; human/mouse only; inaccessible compute scale
primary_source: Nature full text, Results and Methods

data_scope: 57 datasets across human and multispecies region, epigenetic, expression, variant and TAD tasks
objective: frozen embeddings plus downstream random forest or other probes
architecture: compares DNABERT-2, GROVER, NT-v2, HyenaDNA and Caduceus
training_compute: inference runtime measured on one A100
split_or_leakage_control: chromosome-nested CV only for QTLs; many classification sets use random 70:30 splits because chromosome metadata are absent
key_evidence: multispecies Hyena pretraining improves 14 of 49 tasks; Caduceus is robust; simple CNN often beats frozen FMs
limitation: many random splits can overstate genomic generalization; frozen-vs-trained CNN is not fully symmetric
primary_source: Nature Communications full text, Results and Methods

data_scope: multispecies alignment for genome-wide human variant prediction
objective: likelihood-based variant scoring with alignment-informed pretraining
split_or_leakage_control: formal alignment and variant benchmarks; details require subscription full text
key_evidence: alignment-informed model is a strong variant-effect comparator
limitation: brief communication; detailed architecture not extracted from open full text
primary_source: Nature Biotechnology verified metadata and abstract

split_or_leakage_control: published downstream splits must be re-audited before comparison
key_evidence: demonstrates subquadratic long-context DNA modeling at single-base resolution
limitation: human-only pretraining and no explicit RC equivariance

data_scope: human reference genome in released pretrained variants
architecture: Mamba-based bidirectional model with RC parameter sharing/equivariance
split_or_leakage_control: published downstream splits must be re-audited before comparison
key_evidence: combines efficient state-space sequence modeling with exact RC treatment
limitation: not plant-specific; external task overlap requires leakage audit

data_scope: plant genomes; detailed corpus not extracted from accessible metadata
architecture: reported multiscale integration; details not extracted
key_evidence: current plant-specific comparator for discovery and generation
limitation: very recent paper; architecture and benchmark claims require full-text/code audit before use
